key_points: Media plays a critical role in shaping public opinion by selecting and presenting information
Corporate ownership and advertising revenue can influence the type of news and programs shown
The media acts as a watchdog by investigating corruption and holding authorities accountable
Freedom of the press is essential for a healthy democracy because it allows independent reporting
Media literacy helps people critically analyze news and distinguish facts from biased information
examples: Investigative journalism exposing misuse of public funds demonstrates media accountability
Debates on television about government policies encourage public discussion
Advertising strategies during prime-time television programs target large audiences
Social media platforms spreading viral news stories show the speed of modern communication
News reports on environmental issues can influence public awareness and policy discussions
formulas: Watchdog Role → Media monitoring authorities and exposing wrongdoing
Media Bias → When information is presented in a way that favors one perspective
Media Literacy → Ability to critically evaluate and interpret media messages
exam_tips: Focus on how media ownership and advertising influence news content
Understand the importance of independent media in protecting democracy
Remember examples of investigative journalism and the watchdog function
Practice analyzing how media can shape or influence public perception
